verdict_reasoning: BAX is a pro-apoptotic BCL2-family member, canonically cytosolic and mitochondrial-outer-membrane resident. However, BAX undergoes cell-death-induced surface externalization during apoptosis and other forms of programmed cell death (apoptosis, necroptosis); cytosolic BAX translocates to the PM outer leaflet and is exposed on dying cells. This qualifies as contextual surface display via cell-state-induced mechanism. Additionally, BAX has been reported as a target for some immuno-therapeutic modalities that engage it on the surface of apoptotic / dying cells.
